title: Troubleshooting
description: Recover from you-agent-factory install, run, configuration, MCP, and dynamic-workflow failures with short symptom-scoped guidance that links to the owning canonical docs.
openingSummary: Troubleshooting is the you-agent-factory recovery lookup for recognizable failure symptoms. Each entry gives a short action you can try, then points at the canonical doc that owns the full contract.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Troubleshooting is the you-agent-factory recovery lookup for install, first-run, configuration, MCP, and dynamic-workflow failures. Use it when something fails and you need a scannable symptom, a short recovery action, and a link into the owning canonical doc rather than a second copy of that doc.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A failure entry pairs a recognizable symptom with a short recovery action and a pointer to the canonical documentation page that owns the deeper contract. Recovery stays symptom-scoped: try the short action first, then open install, getting started, CLI, configuration, MCP, dynamic workflows, or Factory Session when you need the full reference. This surface does not replace those pages.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Scan for the symptom that matches what you see, try the short recovery action, then follow the link into the owning canonical doc when you need commands, flags, or deeper setup. Use this page for recovery lookup. Open the linked install, getting-started, CLI, configuration, MCP, dynamic-workflows, or Factory Session pages when you need the full contract those surfaces own.

commandNotFound.title: you Not Found After Install
commandNotFound.body: Symptom: your shell reports you: command not found, or which you finds nothing, after you ran an install script. Recovery: open a new terminal so PATH reloads, then confirm you resolves. If the binary is still missing, re-run the install script for your operating system and confirm the script finished without error before retrying you. For the full OS install paths and post-install scaffold notes, open Install.

osInstallOrInit.title: OS Install Or Executor Init Confusion
osInstallOrInit.body: Symptom: the install path does not match your OS, or you are unsure which you init starter scaffold to use after install. Recovery: use the macOS/Linux curl script or the Windows PowerShell script for your machine, then scaffold with you init for the default Codex-backed starter, or you init --executor claude when you want a Claude-backed starter. Do not mix OS install entrypoints. For the install reference and first-run order, open Install and Getting Started.

noLiveFactory.title: Submit Or Work Against A Factory That Is Not Live
noLiveFactory.body: Symptom: you session list is empty, or the host connection fails, when you try to submit or inspect work. Recovery: start or attach a factory with you or you run first, then confirm a live Factory Session with you session list before you submit. An empty list means the host responded but no session is registered yet; a connection failure means nothing is listening—start the factory and retry. For session liveness and the broader command matrix, open Factory Session, Getting Started, and CLI.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web recovery lookup for you-agent-factory failure modes. It is not the install command matrix, not the getting-started quickstart, not a full CLI flag dump, not the configuration or global-configuration-factories deep dive, not MCP host setup, not dynamic-workflow authoring, and not a sync of packaged you docs. Sibling links aid discovery only; they are not required to understand the short recovery actions here.
